Alex is a reader who prefers to stick to traditional values and beliefs. However, they have recently discovered a new interest in exploring different ideas about love and relationships. This shift in perspective has led Alex to pick up three novels that challenge their previous assumptions. Each of these novels has a unique story and approach to the topic of love. The first novel that Alex read had 120 pages and took four days to finish. This book introduced Alex to a new way of thinking about relationships. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages, and it took six days to complete. This novel expanded Alex's understanding of love in various forms. The third novel was even longer, with 240 pages, and it required eight days to finish. This book provided Alex with a deeper insight into the complexities of human connections.  

To calculate the average number of pages Alex read per day, we need to consider the total number of pages and the total number of days spent reading. The total number of pages across all three novels is 120 plus 180 plus 240. That adds up to 540 pages in total. The total number of days spent reading is four plus six plus eight. That equals 18 days in total. If Alex read the same number of pages each day for each novel, then the average number of pages read per day can be calculated by dividing the total pages by the total days. This gives us 540 divided by 18, which equals 30. So, Alex read an average of 30 pages per day.  

This average is based on the assumption that Alex read the same number of pages each day for each novel. It also assumes that there were no days when Alex did not read at all. The calculation includes all three novels, regardless of their length or the time they took to finish. The result is a consistent daily reading rate that applies to each book. This average helps to provide a clear picture of Alex's reading habits. It shows how much time Alex invested in each novel and how much they managed to read each day.  

Each novel had a different number of pages and a different number of days. The first novel required four days to complete, while the second took six days. The third novel was the longest, requiring eight days. Despite these differences, Alex maintained a consistent reading pace.
